title: Suspicious Outbound Traffic — Data Exfiltration
story: Network monitoring flagged an unusually large volume of outbound traffic from
an internal database server to an external IP address.
The destination IP has no business justification, and the transfer occurred
during off-hours. Total data transferred is in the gigabyte range.
Identify the internal source, external destination, protocol, and volume.
hints: Firewall logs show src/dst pairs with byte counts
Use: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case7_* | rex ... | stats sum(bytes) by dst_ip
The transfer used a non-standard port — not 80 or 443
Filter by destination NOT in RFC1918 private ranges to find external traffic
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case7_* | rex field=_raw "SRC=(?<src>[\d.]+).*DST=(?<dst>[\d.]+).*DPT=(?<dpt>\d+).*LEN=(?<len>\d+)" | stats sum(len) as total_bytes by src, dst, dpt | sort -total_bytes
